symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained excessive postural tachycardia (>30 bpm rise) with presyncope, resolving supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained >30 bpm postural heart-rate rise without hypotension is the defining feature of POTS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stable/rising blood pressure on standing (no hypotension)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A stable/rising blood pressure excludes orthostatic hypotension and supports POTS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dependent acrocyanosis of the legs on standing, resolving supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dependent acrocyanosis from venous pooling is a recognised POTS feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic fatigue, exercise intolerance, tremulousness and brain fog

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue, exercise intolerance, tremulousness and brain fog are common POTS symptoms.

symptoms.symptom_name: No true syncope; tachycardia does not persist supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of true syncope and of supine tachycardia argues against a primary arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Standardised active-stand/tilt test: sustained >30 bpm rise without hypotension

investigation_details.investigation_name: Standardised active-stand or tilt-table test (with ECG)
investigation_details.expected_result: A sustained heart-rate increase of >=30 bpm within 10 minutes of standing without orthostatic hypotension, confirming POTS
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: POTS is not objectively confirmed and specialist management is not triggered.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A standardised sustained postural tachycardia without hypotension confirms POTS.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: An excessive postural heart-rate rise without a blood-pressure fall, with dependent acrocyanosis and immediate relief supine, indicates POTS - a benign but debilitating dysautonomia managed conservatively with referral, not emergency care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Standardised active-stand or tilt-table test (via GP/specialist)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectively confirms POTS; done in a standardised setting.
Excludes a primary arrhythmia as an alternative cause of the tachycardia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods (full blood count, thyroid function, iron studies)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for contributing factors (anaemia, thyroid dysfunction) and secondary causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance/ED for a stable, red-flag-negative POTS presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Unnecessary over-triage; POTS is managed with conservative measures and referral, reserving emergency care for true syncope or supine tachycardia.
